## Summary

Computes the sign of a 32-bit integer, returning -1 for negative inputs and +1 otherwise.

## Contract

Returns -1 when the input is negative and +1 when the input is zero or positive.

## Notes

This variant never returns 0. It collapses zero into the non-negative branch and produces +1.
The arithmetic-shift implementation is shorter, but it depends on right-shifting signed negatives propagating the sign bit on the target architecture.

## Optimized Implementations

### unsigned_shift

```c
bh_output_t bh_optimized_unsigned_shift(bh_input_t input)
{
    return 1 | -(bh_output_t)((uint32_t)input >> 31);
}
```

### arithmetic_shift

```c
bh_output_t bh_optimized_arithmetic_shift(bh_input_t input)
{
    return 1 | (input >> 31);
}
```

## Reference Implementation

```c
bh_output_t bh_reference(bh_input_t input)
{
    return (input < 0) ? -1 : 1;
}
```
